Title: A dirigent protein redirects extracellular terpenoid metabolism for defense against biotic challenges

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhDP1_A1 is a cotton dirigent protein specifically expressed in secretory glandular cells of green organs and localized to the extracellular cavity/apoplast. 2. Silencing or CRISPR knockout of GhDP1_A1 together with its highly similar paralog GhDP1_A2 abolished hemigossypolone and heliocides and strongly increased gossypol, showing that it redirects hemigossypol away from gossypol formation. 3. Recombinant GhDP1_A1, together with apoplastic factors or GhAKR13D2_A3 under oxidative conditions, promotes hydroxylation of hemigossypol to a hydroxylated intermediate that spontaneously oxidizes to hemigossypolone. 4. Loss of GhDP1 function increases susceptibility to herbivores and fungal pathogens, linking extracellular terpenoid redirection to defense.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dirigent protein, DIR-b/d subfamily, secreted glycoprotein with N-terminal signal peptide
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secretory glandular cells/extracellular cavity of green organs such as leaves and stems for hemigossypolone/heliocides; non-green organs for gossypol
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes hemigossypolone and heliocide accumulation while suppressing flux to gossypol in green organs
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression analysis; scRNA-seq; promoter::GUS/GFP; immunogold localization; VIGS; CRISPR-Cas9 double knockout with GhDP1_A2; apoplastic fluid biochemical assays; recombinant protein assays; site-directed mutagenesis; LC-MS metabolite profiling; insect feeding and fungal infection assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in secretory glandular cells of green organs; negligible in D-subgenome homologs
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Secretory glandular cells in leaves/stems/calyces/pistils; organ pattern correlates with hemigossypolone accumulation
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Apoplast; extracellular cavity of pigment glands
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: scRNA-seq: GEO GSE243419; RNA-seq: PRJNA1210977, PRJNA248163, PRJNA493958; IP-MS proteomics: PXD062712; APF proteomics: PXD062792
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oxidative conditions; Cu2+ or Fe3+; apoplastic fluid; biotic stress by Helicoverpa armigera, Spodoptera frugiperda, Fusarium oxysporum, Rhizoctonia solani, Botrytis cinerea
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No observable growth or development penalty reported in GhDP1 double knockout, but loss of defense metabolites causes increased susceptibility to multiple biotic stresses
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although functionally decisive, GhDP1_A1 is not a classical catalyst and is best categorized as a common metabolic gatekeeper rather than pathway enzyme or transcriptional regulator. R145 and Y102 are essential residues for activity.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhDP1_A2 is a paralog of GhDP1_A1 with ~93% sequence identity and similar secretory glandular cell-specific, extracellular localization. 2. Joint silencing or knockout with GhDP1_A1 eliminates hemigossypolone and heliocides and increases gossypol in green organs. 3. Recombinant GhDP1_A2 functionally mimics GhDP1_A1 in apoplastic assays, promoting hemigossypol hydroxylation toward hemigossypolone in the presence of apoplastic factors.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dirigent protein, DIR-b/d subfamily, secreted glycoprotein with N-terminal signal peptide
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secretory glandular cells/extracellular cavity of green organs
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes hemigossypolone and heliocide production and redirects metabolism away from gossypol
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression analysis; promoter::GUS/GFP; immunogold localization; VIGS; CRISPR-Cas9 double knockout with GhDP1_A1; recombinant protein apoplastic assays; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in secretory glandular cells of green organs
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Gland-specific and positively correlated with hemigossypolone accumulation
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Apoplast; extracellular cavity of pigment glands
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GhAKR13D2_A3 inferred via paralogous functional equivalence, but direct interaction shown for GhDP1_A1
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against herbivores and pathogens via hemigossypolone/heliocides
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct biochemical equivalence to GhDP1_A1 was supported in N. tabacum APF assays; D-subgenome homologs show negligible expression.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhAKR13D2_A3 is an aldo-keto reductase that physically interacts with GhDP1_A1 and is strongly coexpressed with GhDP1_A1 and hemigossypolone accumulation. 2. VIGS of the GhAKR13D2 family and specific CRISPR knockout of GhAKR13D2_A3 significantly reduce hemigossypolone and heliocides in cotton, showing a critical role in this pathway. 3. GhAKR13D2_A3 cooperates with GhDP1_A1 under oxidative conditions to enhance hydroxylation of hemigossypol to the hydroxylated intermediate, despite canonical NADPH addition inhibiting this reaction. 4. The protein localizes partly to the plasma membrane and apoplast, indicating an unconventional extracellular role for an AKR.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Green organs, especially leaves and stems; extracellular cavity/apoplast of glandular tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for efficient accumulation of hemigossypolone and heliocides
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: IP-MS candidate screening; split luciferase complementation; BiFC; pull-down; co-IP; scRNA-seq; immunogold labeling; apoplastic proteomics; VIGS; RNA-seq; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; in vitro conjoint enzymatic assays with GhDP1_A1; LC-MS metabolite profiling; Biacore and AKR activity assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in secretory glandular cells and green organs such as leaves and stems; low in roots and ovules
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elevated in photosynthetically active green organs where hemigossypolone and heliocides accumulate
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA1210977, PRJNA248163, PRJNA493958; scRNA-seq: GEO GSE243419; IP-MS proteomics: PXD062712; APF proteomics: PXD062792
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oxidative conditions; Cu2+ or Fe3+; NADPH inhibits the GhDP1_A1-dependent oxidation reaction
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against herbivores and pathogens via production of hemigossypolone and heliocides
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Discussion notes GhAKR13D2 perturbation may also reduce gossypol, implying broader metabolic roles beyond hemigossypolone branch
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Atypical for AKRs, which are usually intracellular and NADPH-dependent; here it acts in extracellular oxidative chemistry with GhDP1.
